entities.name: GULLY EROSION
entities.description: A widespread problem of soil erosion characterized by the formation of gullies, common in semi-arid regions

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGION
entities.description: A region characterized by denuded landscapes and flash floods where gully erosion is common

entities.name: DENUDED LANDSCAPE
entities.description: A landscape stripped of vegetation, contributing to gully erosion in semi-arid regions

entities.name: FLASH FLOODS
entities.description: Sudden, intense floods that contribute to gully erosion in semi-arid regions

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: A country where an estimated 4 million hectares of land are affected by severe gully erosion

entities.name: AFRICA
entities.description: A continent where an estimated 29 million hectares of land are affected by severe gully erosion

entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The general process of soil displacement, of which gully erosion is a more difficult and expensive type to control

entities.name: GULLY STABILIZATION
entities.description: The goal of achieving stability in gullies through an understanding of erosion processes

entities.name: GULLY FORMATION
entities.description: The process and causes leading to the creation of gullies

entities.name: WATERSHED DEVELOPMENT AND MANAGEMENT
entities.description: The overall framework within which gully control is implemented

entities.name: GULLY CONTROL STRUCTURES
entities.description: Physical interventions designed to control and stabilize gully erosion

entities.description: A specific type of gully control structure

entities.description: A specific type of gully control structure

entities.description: A specific type of gully control structure

entities.description: Hydrological data used for the selection, design, and construction of gully control structures
